strengths: Maintained consistent traction throughout the cutting sequence
Completed the full circle within reasonable time parameters
Demonstrated good instrument coordination during the cutting phases
weaknesses: Cut deviates noticeably from the marked circle at several points
Inconsistent cutting angle causing irregular path following
Traction positioning could be optimized for better scissor entry angle

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, pausing between each quarter to reset positioning and verify alignment with the marked line.
drills.targets_weakness: Cut deviates noticeably from the marked circle at several points

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle, focusing on maintaining optimal cutting geometry throughout the circle.
drills.targets_weakness: Traction positioning could be optimized for better scissor entry angle

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your consistent completion into precise path following. Your time of 93 seconds is approaching proficiency - now prioritize accuracy over speed to eliminate line deviations.
